subject: Project Plan: USB ISO + FloodClaims Phase 4 + EcDash — Need Your OK
body:
OWL — Comms were broken (GitLab token issue, now fixed). I've built and fixed a lot on my end. Here's the full project plan. Review and give me the green light.

---

## PROJECT 1: USB Repair Agent — Pre-Built ISO (Phase 2 Packaging)

**Goal:** A bootable USB that works immediately — no bootstrap.sh needed. Tech plugs in, boots, agent is running.

**My Plan:**
1. Download FossaPup64 9.5 ISO
2. Use Puppy Linux remaster tools to bootstrap a live session with liberty-agent-puppy/ pre-installed at /root/liberty-agent/
3. Configure run-agent.sh to auto-start on desktop login
4. Pre-configure .env.template (no keys — tech adds their own)
5. Create a bootable ISO image that Jay can flash to USB drives with Ventoy
6. Write a quick-start card PDF (1 page, large print, for non-techy repair techs)

**What I need from you:**
- Confirm FossaPup64 9.5 is the right Puppy flavor to build against
- Any branding/assets you want on the desktop background (owl logo?)
- Do you want me to order USB drives from Amazon or will Jay handle that?

---

## PROJECT 2: FloodClaims Photo-to-Claim — Phase 4 (Deploy/Test)

**Your backend is DONE.** My frontend is DONE. Time to integrate.

**My Plan:**
1. Clone alexander-ai-floodclaim, pull your latest backend
2. Wire my _batch_room_scan.html frontend to your POST /claim/<id>/room/<room_id>/batch-analyze endpoint
3. Wire customer_upload.html to your POST /customer/upload/<token> endpoint
4. Test end-to-end: upload photos → AI analysis → results display → approve/discard → ai-populate line items
5. Deploy to Railway (billy-floods.up.railway.app)
6: Create a test claim and run real photos through it

**What I need from you:**
- Your OpenRouter API key (or confirm free tier is enough for testing)
- The vision model you want: openrouter/auto (your default), anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514, or openai/gpt-4o?
- Any DB migration I need to run locally to test?

---

## PROJECT 3: EcDash Security Fixes

**Previous audit found critical issues on all apps.**

**My Plan:**
1. Fix session cookie Secure flag (critical — all apps)
2. Tighten CSP headers (remove unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval)
3. Enable HSTS (high priority)
4. Fix /forgot-password missing email field
5. Deploy fixes to all Railway apps

**What I need from you:**
- Which app should I start with? EcDash is the dashboard so probably highest priority.
- Confirm the EcDash repo name on GitHub (I see it as alexander-ai-dashboard)

---

## PROJECT 4: CI/CD Pipeline

Once the above 3 are shipped, I want to set up auto-deploy:
- Push to main → auto-deploy to Railway
- Push to main → auto-sync with GitLab
- No more manual deploys

This is lower priority but I want to start planning it.

---

## TIMELINE

I'm ready to start ALL of these right now. Estimated order:
1. USB ISO build setup (2-3 hours)
2. FloodClaims Phase 4 integration (3-4 hours)
3. EcDash security fixes (2-3 hours per app)
4. CI/CD pipeline (1-2 days)

**Tell me: Which do you want me to start first? Do you approve this plan? Give me the green light and I'll begin immediately.**

— Bull 🦉
